definition: Carry-to-vol ratios in G10 are systematically overstated: nominal ratios of 0.9-1.1 drop to regime-adjusted 0.5-0.7. The regime-conditional R-squared collapses from 8-12% to 0-2% (Chow test F=7.2). Implied volatility systematically understates realized volatility during drawdowns, making carry appear to offer sufficient compensation when it does not.
evidence: Regime-conditional R-squared collapse from 8-12% to 0-2%, Chow test F=7.2
Implied vol systematically understates realized vol during drawdowns
Agricultural sticky floor mechanically widens carry attractiveness when EM fundamentals deteriorate
Carry looks cheap exactly when it is most dangerous
open_questions: Is the regime-adjusted ratio stable across different stress episodes?
Can regime-conditional carry-to-vol be estimated in real-time with sufficient accuracy to be tradeable?
How does the agricultural sticky floor interact with carry mispricing in EM?
